title: Intro to Assembly Language
html:
Intro to Assembly Language

Registers

64-bit Register (8-bytes)

8-bit Register (1-bytes)

rax

al

rbx

bl

rdi

dil

rsi

sil

rdx

dl

rcx

cl

r8

r8b

r9

r9b

rbp

bpl

rsp

spl

rip

ipl

Assembly and Disassembly

Assemble code

nasm -f elf64 helloWorld.s

Link code

ld -o helloWorld helloWorld.o

Link code with libc functions

ld -o fib fib.o -lc --dynamic-linker /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2

Disassemble .text section

objdump -M intel -d helloWorld

Show binary assembly code

objdump -M intel --no-show-raw-insn --no-addresses -d helloWorld

Disassemble .data section

objdump -sj .data helloWorld

GDB

Open binary in gdb

gdb -q ./helloWorld

View binary functions

info functions

View binary variables

info variables

View registers

registers

Disassemble label/function

disas _start

Break label/function

b _start

Break address

b *0x401000

Run the binary

r

Examine register "x/ count-format-size $register"

x/4xg $rip

Step to the next instruction

si

Step to the next line of code

s

Step to the next function

ni

Continue to the next break point

c

Patch address value

patch string 0x402000 "Patched!\\x0a"

Set register value

set $rdx=0x9

Assembly Instructions

Move data or load immediate data

`mov rax, 1` -> `rax = 1`

Load an address pointing to the value

`lea rax, [rsp+5]` -> `rax = rsp+5 `

Swap data between two registers or addresses

`xchg rax, rbx` -> `rax = rbx, rbx = rax`

Increment by 1

`inc rax` -> `rax++` or `rax += 1` -> `rax = 2`

Decrement by 1

`dec rax` -> `rax--` or `rax -= 1` -> `rax = 0`

Add both operands

`add rax, rbx` -> `rax = 1 + 1` -> `2`

Subtract Source from Destination (i.e rax = rax - rbx)

`sub rax, rbx` -> `rax = 1 - 1` -> `0`

Multiply both operands

`imul rax, rbx` -> `rax = 1 * 1` -> `1`

Bitwise NOT (invert all bits, 0->1 and 1->0)

`not rax` -> `NOT 00000001` -> `11111110`

Bitwise AND (if both bits are 1 -> 1, if bits are different -> 0)

`and rax, rbx` -> `00000001 AND 00000010` -> `00000000`

Bitwise OR (if either bit is 1 -> 1, if both are 0 -> 0)

`or rax, rbx` -> `00000001 OR 00000010` -> `00000011`

Bitwise XOR (if bits are the same -> 0, if bits are different -> 1)

`xor rax, rbx` -> `00000001 XOR 00000010` -> `00000011`

Sets loop (rcx) counter to x

mov rcx, 3

Jumps back to the start of loop until counter reaches 0

loop exampleLoop

Jumps to specified label, address, or location

jmp loop

Destination equal to Zero

D = 0

Destination Not equal to Zero

D != 0

Destination is Negative

D < 0

Destination is Not Negative (i.e. 0 or positive)

D >= 0

Destination Greater than Source

D > S

Destination Greater than or Equal Source

D >= S

Destination Less than Source

D < S

Destination Less than or Equal Source

D <= S

Sets RFLAGS by subtracting second operand from first operand (i.e. first - second)

`cmp rax, rbx` -> `rax - rbx`

Copies the specified register/address to the top of the stack

push rax

Moves the item at the top of the stack to the specified register/address

pop rax

push the next instruction pointer rip to the stack, then jumps to the specified procedure

call printMessage

pop the address at rsp into rip, then jump to it

ret

Functions

Locate write syscall number

cat /usr/include/x86_64-linux-gnu/asm/unistd_64.h | grep write

write syscall man page

man -s 2 write

printf libc man page

man -s 3 printf

Syscall Calling Convention 1. Save registers to stack 2. Set its syscall number in rax 3. Set its arguments in the registers 4. Use the syscall assembly instruction to call it Function Calling Convention 1. Save Registers on the stack (Caller Saved) 2. Pass Function Arguments (like syscalls) 3. Fix Stack Alignment 3. Get Function's Return Value (in rax)

Shellcoding

Instruction to shellcode

pwn asm 'push rax'  -c 'amd64'

Shellcode to instructions

pwn disasm '50' -c 'amd64'

Extract binary shellcode

python3 shellcoder.py helloworld

Run shellcode

python3 loader.py '4831..0f05

Assemble shellcode into binary

python assembler.py '4831..0f05

List available syscalls

pwn shellcraft -l 'amd64.linux'

Generate syscalls shellcode

pwn shellcraft amd64.linux.sh

Run syscalls shellcode

pwn shellcraft amd64.linux.sh -r

List available syscalls

msfvenom -l payloads | grep 'linux/x64'

Generate syscalls shellcode

msfvenom -p 'linux/x64/exec' CMD='sh' -a 'x64' --platform 'linux' -f 'hex'

Generate encoded syscalls shellcode

msfvenom -p 'linux/x64/exec' CMD='sh' -a 'x64' --platform 'linux' -f 'hex' -e 'x64/xor'

Shellcoding Requirements 1. Does not contain variables 2. Does not refer to direct memory addresses 3. Does not contain any NULL bytes 00
